acquirers.name: Duncan Holdco III LLC
acquirers.identifier: State of Delaware Company File Number  10510961

deals_cleared.merger_description: Duncan Holdco III LLC, a holding company that is ultimately controlled by SoftBank Group Corp. (Japan) (**SoftBank**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of common stock (on a fully diluted basis) of, and voting rights in, DigitalBridge Group Inc. (**DigitalBridge**) (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Duncan Holdco III LLC (Duncan Holdco 3) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued
and outstanding shares of common stock (on a fully diluted basis)
of, and voting rights in, DigitalBridge Group Inc. (DigitalBridge)
through 2 coordinated reverse subsidiary mergers, as described in
the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition). Duncan Holdco 3 is ultimately a subsidiary of SoftBank
Group Corp. (Japan) (SoftBank).
The Acquisition involves Duncan Sub I Inc., a wholly owned
subsidiary of Duncan Holdco 3, merging with and into DigitalBridge,
with DigitalBridge surviving as a wholly owned direct subsidiary of
Duncan Holdco 3. Immediately following, Duncan Sub II LLC, a wholly
owned subsidiary of Duncan Sub I Inc., will merge with and into
DigitalBridge Operating Company, LLC, with DigitalBridge Operating
Company, LLC surviving as a direct subsidiary of DigitalBridge.
As a result of the Acquisition, each of DigitalBridge and DigitalBridge
Operating Company, LLC will become solely controlled, indirect
subsidiaries of SoftBank.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 March 2026

SoftBank is a publicly traded global investor headquartered in Tokyo,
Japan, with a varied portfolio of investments and subsidiaries
focused on the technology sector. Companies and investments
controlled by SoftBank conduct business activities globally across a
wide range of sectors, including online learning, mobile and fixed-line
communications services, microelectronics, transportation,
software-as-a-service, internet advertising and e-commerce, banking,
payment and investment, IT platforms, artificial intelligence and
mobile robots.
The acquirer, Duncan Holdco 3, is a holding company created for the
purposes of the Acquisition and is ultimately a subsidiary controlled
by SoftBank.
The target, DigitalBridge, is a dedicated, global-scale digital
infrastructure investment firm that invests, directly and through its
managed funds and their portfolio companies, in data centres, cell
towers, fibre networks, small cells, and edge infrastructure.
DigitalBridge is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida, United States
of America with key offices in New York, Los Angeles, London,
Luxembourg and Singapore. DigitalBridge is publicly listed on the
New York Stock Exchange.
Both SoftBank and DigitalBridge have connected entities that supply
internet connectivity services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between the connected
entities of SoftBank and DigitalBridge in the supply of
internet connectivity services in Australia
b. SoftBank and DigitalBridge have a small presence in the
supply of internet connectivity services in Australia
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of internet connectivity
services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_name: AZ Next Generation Advisory – Intend Financial

acquirers.name: AZ NEXT GENERATION ADVISORY PTY LTD

deals_cleared.merger_description: AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (**AZ Next Generation Advisory**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in BMM Partners Pty Ltd, trading as Intend Financial (**Intend Financial**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (AZ Next Generation Advisory)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the share capital in BMM Partners Pty Ltd
(Intend Financial), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 March 2026

The acquirer, AZ Next Generation Advisory, is an Australian
professional services advisory company established in 2014 that
owns approximately 48 financial planning and accounting
businesses with operations and customers across Australia,
including in Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania (where Intend
Financial has a presence). AZ Next Generation Advisory has
approximately 404 licensed financial planners across its network.
AZ Next Generation Advisory is a subsidiary of AZ Aust Holdings Pty
Ltd whose major shareholders are funds managed by Oaktree
Capital Management LP (Oaktree) and Azimut Holding S.p.a.
(Azimut). Oaktree is a global investment management firm and
Azimut is a global asset and wealth management business.
The target, Intend Financial, is a financial planning business
established in 2021 that provides financial planning services from
four offices, in Victoria (Mornington and Hawthorn East), Queensland
(Gold Coast) and Tasmania (Launceston), to customers across
Australia. Intend Financial has 10 licensed financial planners.
AZ Next Generation Advisory and Intend Financial both supply
financial planning services, which include wealth management,
retirement planning, insurance, investment, estate and succession,
debt management, business planning and superannuation advisory
services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between AZ Next
Generation Advisory and Intend Financial in the supply of
financial planning services in Northern Tasmania, Gold
Coast, Greater Melbourne and across Australia
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial
planning services in the relevant geographic areas is
estimated to be low and the increase in share of supply
arising from the Acquisition would be small
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of financial planning
services in Northern Tasmania, Gold Coast, Greater
Melbourne and across Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Hall & McLean – Freehold interest in commercial land in Wellcamp, QLD
deals_cleared.merger_description: Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (**Hall & McLean**) proposes to acquire a 100% freehold interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp, QLD 4350 (the **Property**), from Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**) (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (Hall & McLean) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a 100% freehold
interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known
as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp QLD 4350 (the Property), from
Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the Vendor), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 March 2026

The acquirer, Hall & McLean, is a 50:50 joint venture between
Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd (Ellerslie) and the McLean family
(through Alpair Pty Ltd).
Alpair Pty Ltd, is an egg producer based in the Darling Downs region
in Queensland.
Ellerslie is an agribusiness entity operating principally in the chicken
egg production sector. Ellerslie layer farms produce caged, cage
free, free range, pasture raised and organic shell eggs.
Ellerslie is 60% owned by PSP Investments, with the remaining 40%
held by DHP Consolidated Pty Ltd on behalf of the Hall family.
Ellerslie and Alpair Pty Ltd each have a 50% interest in Sunny Queen,
an egg distributor.
Ellerslie has been renting the Property from the Vendor under a 5-
year lease for the purposes of egg grading and cold storage for its
egg production business.
The Vendor does not operate any egg farming or production
business in Australia.
The Property would be an input into the operations of Ellerslie.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition is an acquisition of land by an egg producer
from a party who is not an egg producer
b. Ellerslie has been using the land as a lessee for 5 years and
intends to continue using the land for the same purposes,
but as an owner
c. there appear to be alternative sites available for Ellerslie’s
and the McLean family’s competitors
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-20008/Hall and McLean - Freehold interest in commercial land in Wellcamp, QLD (WA-20008) - Notification waiver determination - 23 March 2026.pdf

deals_cleared.merger_description: Five V Capital Pty Ltd (**Five V Capital**) is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. Five V Capital manages a number of Five V funds, including Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund. Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund are investment funds that pursue a strategy of investing in private mid-market businesses.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, Five V Capital Pty Ltd and Five V Horizons
Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of Overwatch
Bidco Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of the majority of the issued
shares in FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (the FTP
Group), as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 March 2026

The acquirer, Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, is indirectly wholly owned by
Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund, investment funds managed by Five
V Capital Pty Ltd (Five V Capital). Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund
pursue strategies of investing in private mid-market businesses. Five
V Capital is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture
capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. It
manages a number of Five V funds which have investments in a wide
range of businesses and industries.
The target, FTP Group, is based in Australia, Canada and New
Zealand and operates under the business names ‘FTP Solutions’,
‘Faarm’ and ‘Yarn Mesh’. FTP Solutions provides integrated network
and operational monitoring software to the global mining industry.
Faarm is a software platform that aggregates disparate farm data
into a single interface to enable farmers to monitor farms. Yarn
Mesh designs and manufactures long-range mesh networking
hardware that delivers connectivity across remote environments.
A connected entity of Five V Capital, Beacon TopCo Pty Ltd trading
as Digital Matter (Digital Matter), supplies IoT hardware solutions
and device management software that enable long-term asset
tracking, sensor monitoring and telematics applications. Digital
Matter’s solutions can be used in several settings, including in the
agricultural setting, for example for remote sensor monitoring,
environmental monitoring, and water monitoring.
Broadly, Digital Matter and FTP Group’s Faarm business (Faarm)
both supply solutions that facilitate remote monitoring for use in
agricultural settings. Digital Matter supplies hardware devices (with
some associated software such as device management software),
whereas Faarm offers a platform that integrates with third party
hardware.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between FTP Group and Five V Capital and its connected
entities.
b. There are several other suppliers of agricultural monitoring
solutions and of IoT hardware solutions and device
management software in Australia.
c. Faarm has a relatively small presence in Australia.
d. While there is a potential complementary relationship
between Digital Matter and Faarm in the supply of
monitoring solutions for use in agricultural settings, there is
a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct and/or bundling or tying of products
post-Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-25008/Five V Capital - FTP Group (WA-25008) - Notification waiver determination - 24 March 2026.pdf

deals_cleared.merger_description: On 27 February 2026, Rocket Purchaser, L.L.C. (an affiliate of various funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc. (**Blackstone**)) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger to acquire all the issued shares in Advanced Cooling Technologies, Inc. (**ACT**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Rocket Purchaser, L.L.C. (Rocket) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued shares in
Advanced Cooling Technologies, Inc. (ACT), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 March 2026

Blackstone, Inc. (Blackstone) is a US-based investment management
firm. Rocket is a special purpose vehicle that is an affiliate of various
funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone.
The target, ACT, is a US-based engineering and manufacturing
company specialising in advanced thermal management and cooling
solutions. It supplies passive and active heat transfer technologies,
including heat pipes, two-phase and pumped liquid cooling loops,
thermal storage devices, and custom engineered systems for
applications in aerospace, defence, spacecraft, electronics, data
centres, HVAC, medical devices, energy recovery and industrial
markets.
The application indicates that no funds advised and/or managed by
affiliates of Blackstone have an interest in any business that
competes in relation to any of the products supplied by ACT in
Australia, or acquires products from, or supplies products or services
to, ACT in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a) There is no horizontal overlap in Australia between the
products and services supplied by the parties, and there are
no vertical relationships.
b) ACT has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: IOR Pty Ltd proposes to acquire the business assets of Delta Specialised Energy Pty Ltd in respect of its diesel, AdBlue and renewable fuels distribution businesses (including the associated plant and equipment and intellectual property).

events.title: IOR - Delta Specialised Energy assets - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
IOR Pty Ltd (IOR) proposes to acquire the business of Delta
Specialised Energy Pty Ltd (Delta) (together, the Parties) in respect
of its diesel, AdBlue and renewable fuels distribution businesses
(including the associated plant and equipment and intellectual
property) through a business sale agreement (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, IOR, is an Australian-based supplier of fuel and
integrated fuel solutions across Australia. It is majority owned by US
based infrastructure investment firm Stonepeak Partners L.P.
The target, Delta, is an Australian-based distributor of diesel, AdBlue
and renewable fuels. It primarily operates in NSW, with materially
smaller activities in the ACT and QLD.

The Parties overlap in the bulk delivery of diesel and AdBlue to
industrial and commercial businesses in NSW. Diesel fuel is distillate
fuel oil used in diesel engines. AdBlue is the marketing name for a
product known as diesel exhaust fluid that is used to reduce
nitrogen oxide emissions which are produced in higher amounts by
diesel engines.
Both diesel and Adblue can be delivered to customers by:
• Using vendor or customer fuel storage tanks, which are
placed onsite with the customer, and replenished when the
tank is running low;
• Delivery of fuel direct into a customer vehicle;
• Self-service at unmanned truck stops or traditional service
stations.
IOR supplies customers via unmanned truck stops and service
stations, whereas Delta does not. Conversely, IOR does not supply
renewable fuels, whereas Delta does.
While IOR and Delta have a vertical relationship in QLD, Delta’s
activities in QLD are minimal. IOR and Delta do not have a vertical
relationship in NSW.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and third parties.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Parties’ market share aggregation is estimated to be low on
any market definition, with an estimated post-acquisition market
share of below 10%.
• The Parties would continue to face competition from alternative
suppliers of bulk fuel delivery in NSW, such as Ampol, BP and
Mobil.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

deals_cleared.merger_name: VFMC – CorVal Land and Operator Trusts
deals_cleared.merger_description: Victorian Funds Management Corporation (**VFMC**), as investment manager of several investment vehicles (**VFMC Clients**), proposes for the VFMC Clients to subscribe to units in two stapled trusts of which CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee, comprising RF CorVal MHE Land Trust (**Land Trust**) and RF CorVal MHE Operator Trust (**Operator Trust**) (together, **MHE Fund**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Victorian Funds Management Corporation (VFMC), as investment
manager of the following investment vehicles:
• the Emergency Services Superannuation Board (as trustee
for Emergency Services Superannuation Scheme)
• the Transport Accident Commission
• the Victorian Managed Insurance Authority
• the Victorian WorkCover Authority, and
• the VFMC Investment Fund (of which VFMC is the trustee)
(together, the VFMC Clients),
proposes that the VFMC Clients subscribe to units in two stapled
trusts of which CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee, comprising
RF CorVal MHE Land Trust (Land Trust) and RF CorVal MHE
Operator Trust (Operator Trust) (together, the MHE Fund), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
As part of the Acquisition, State Street Australia Ltd (State Street),
acting as custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, will hold minority
interests in the relevant MHE Fund entities.
A notification waiver application in respect of the Acquisition was
made by VFMC, the Emergency Service Superannuation Board, the
Transport Accident Commission, the Victorian Managed Insurance
Authority and the Victorian WorkCover Authority.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
26 March 2026

VFMC is a statutory authority established under the Victorian Funds
Management Corporation Act 1994 (Vic) to provide investment and
funds management services to public sector insurance and
superannuation agencies.
VFMC acts as investment manager for a number of clients and
invests across a range of asset classes, including Australian and
international equities, infrastructure and property, inflation linked
bonds, diversified fixed interest, non-traditional strategies (hedge
funds, private credit, insurance and others) and cash.
State Street, the entity that will hold the units in the relevant MHE
Fund entities as a custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, provides
financial solutions to institutional investors through investment
servicing and management, and market and data intelligence.
The target, the MHE Fund, is active in the property and management
industry. The MHE Fund is a stapled structure pursuant to which the
Land Trust holds properties and leases them to the Operator Trust.
MHE Fund’s downstream co-venture, also comprised of a stapled
land trust and operator trust, develops and manages residential
property. CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee of the MHE Fund.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between the current investments of VFMC and/or the VFMC
Clients and the activities of the MHE Fund
b. there are alternative developers and managers of residential
property and manufactured housing estates in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Infotrust Ltd (ASX:ITS) (**Infotrust**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Catalyst Cyber.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Infotrust Ltd (Infotrust) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Catalyst
Cyber Pty Limited (Catalyst Cyber), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
26 March 2026

The acquirer, Infotrust, is an ASX-listed Australian technology
services group. Its cybersecurity offering includes the supply of
governance, risk and compliance (GRC) and assurance consulting,
penetration testing to identify and remediate cyber vulnerabilities in
networks and systems, and strategic advisory to establish
cybersecurity strategies in line with applicable compliance
standards, and managed information technology services. Infotrust
also resells software and products for advanced security solutions in
connection with its consulting engagements, as well as professional
assurance and advisory services. Its cybersecurity offerings are
primarily directed to commercial and enterprise customers across a
range of industries.
The target, Catalyst Cyber, trading as Catalyst Security, is a Canberra-
based cybersecurity consultancy business established in 2024.
Catalyst Cyber is primarily focussed on the supply of cybersecurity
services to Federal Government clients. Its core business comprises
GRC and assurance consulting to assist government clients meet
regulatory requirements and industry standards and penetration
testing to advise on compliance with mandatory information
management requirements. Catalyst Cyber resells to a limited
degree third-party government-grade security testing software and
provides professional services covering governance uplift and
accreditation.
Both Infotrust and Catalyst Cyber provide GRC and assurance
consulting, penetration testing, cyber consulting, software and
product resale, and other professional cybersecurity services in
Australia

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Infotrust and
Catalyst Cyber overlap in the supply of cybersecurity
services in Australia, including a subset of these services.
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of cybersecurity
services to customers in Australia is low and the increase in
share of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of cybersecurity
services in Australia, including in the supply of the subset of
services where there is a horizontal overlap.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: The Michelin Group is a multinational tyre manufacturer and distributor headquartered in Clermont-Ferrand in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France. The Michelin Group is the owner of UK-based Fenner PLC, which includes Fenner America, Inc. Fenner is a manufacturer of engineered products, specifically conveyor solutions and polymer products.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Compagnie Financière Michelin SAS (Michelin) proposes to acquire
100% of the share capital in The Flexitallic Group SAS (Flexitallic),
through the following two steps, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
Specifically, the Acquisition comprises the following steps:
• First, Fenner America, Inc. (Fenner) (a wholly owned entity of
Michelin) will acquire 100% of the shares in FGI Acquisition
Corp. (a wholly owned entity of Flexitallic).
• Second, Michelin will acquire 100% of the shares in
Flexitallic from Flexitallic, S.à r.l., the parent of Flexitallic.
The Acquisition will result in the Michelin Group acquiring full
ownership of Flexitallic.
Michelin and Fenner applied for a notification waiver in respect of
the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
27 March 2026

The acquirers, Michelin and Fenner, are part of the Michelin Group.
The Michelin Group is a multinational tyre manufacturer and
distributor headquartered in Clermont-Ferrand in the Auvergne-
Rhône-Alpes region of France. Fenner is a manufacturer of
engineered products, specifically conveyor solutions and polymer
products.
Globally, the Michelin Group's activities include fall into the following
four areas: tyres; polymer composite solutions (development of
polymer composite solutions and other high-tech materials for
critical applications in industrial applications, mobility, construction,
aeronautics, energy, healthcare and mining; connected solutions
(provision of data and AI based services for professional mobility
and transport operators); and lifestyle (provision of curated travel,
dining and hospitality guides in printed and digital format).
As part of the Michelin Group's ‘polymer composite solutions’, it
manufactures and sells hydraulic seals that are engineered for
dynamic sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between moving parts
such as pistons and rods). In Australia, Michelin supplies sealing
solutions via Hallite Seals Australia.
The target, Flexitallic, is a global manufacturer and supplier of high-
performance sealing solutions that are engineered for static sealing
applications (i.e. the sealing between stationary parts such as
flanged pipe joints and similar joints that do not move or rotate),
specifically spiral wound gaskets, flat gaskets and related materials.
Flexitallic's products are used in stationary pipeline connections and
joints unrelated to cylinders. Flexitallic is headquartered in Paris,
France and has a large global network.
In Australia, Flexitallic focuses on serving the oil and gas, mining,
petrochemical, and power generation industries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Michelin and
Flexitallic in the manufacture and supply of sealing solutions
in Australia
b. the presence of alternative manufacturers and suppliers of
sealing solutions in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Adobe Inc. (**Adobe**) through a newly incorporated direct wholly owned subsidiary, Fenway Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Semrush Holdings, Inc. (Semrush and together with Adobe, the **Parties**). The Proposed Transaction is being implemented pursuant to a merger agreement entered into between Adobe, Merger Sub, and Semrush dated 18 November 2025 (**Merger Agreement**). Pursuant to the Merger Agreement, Merger Sub will merge with and into Semrush, such that Merger Sub will cease to exist and Semrush will become the "surviving corporation" and a direct, wholly owned subsidiary of Adobe. After the Proposed Transaction, Semrush will be solely controlled by Adobe.
